element_introduction_order.element: Basic resource gathering (ore/gems via miner trucks)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Foundation for all economic gameplay; taught before combat
Player learns base protection before facing organized enemy assault

element_introduction_order.element: Faction-specific infantry units (GI for Allies, Conscript for Soviets)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Basic offensive capability; Conscripts cheap but weak; GIs expensive but strong
Soviet vehicles introduce cost/power tradeoff; Allied vehicles emphasize mobility
Introduces map control and economic strategy beyond base refinery
Each nation's asymmetric identity becomes tactically relevant

element_introduction_order.element: Advanced faction units (naval warfare, late-game units)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Allied superiority in late game; requires knowledge of advanced unit counters
One-time economic boost; faction-specific asymmetric ability
Emergency cash source when economy fails; teaches resource desperation

element_introduction_order.element: Random crates on map (chaotic resource/unit bonuses)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Introduces map exploration and luck-based opportunity

typical_relief_triggers: Successful base defense against first wave of attackers
Completion of resource refinery and initial income stabilization
Capture of strategic oil derrick for passive income
Production of first advanced unit counter to enemy force composition
typical_tension_triggers: Initial base vulnerability with limited starting units
Early AI early-game rush attacks (especially Soviet Conscript spam)
Income disruption from miner truck destruction
Shift to mid-game where faction asymmetries become critical
Specific objective missions with defended targets requiring coordinated assault
Multiple simultaneous enemy commander bases on larger maps
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Mission-based progression with escalating complexity; early game emphasizes economic survival and basic defense, mid-game introduces faction asymmetry and strategic positioning, late game shifts to advanced unit composition and multi-pronged attacks

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Oil derricks scattered across map as optional economic targets; players can choose to secure or ignore based on base safety and strategic priority

difficulty_curve.early_game: Low difficulty with economy-focused tutorial; player starts with limited base or Mobile Construction Unit and small unit platoon; AI provides controlled threat
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Moderate-to-high difficulty as faction asymmetries become tactically critical; Soviet early-game tank superiority gives way to Allied versatility; economic efficiency directly impacts unit production rate
difficulty_curve.late_game: High difficulty favoring faction-specific mastery; Allies gain advantage with advanced naval and air units; Soviet players must optimize early-game economic snowball or lose late-game arms race
notable_spikes: Early AI rush attacks (especially Soviet Conscript swarms in online games)
Transition missions with specific non-trivial objectives beyond standard base elimination
Multi-enemy commander maps requiring simultaneous defense and offense

notable_level_patterns.description: Initial mission emphasizes harvesting ore/gems and building refinery before meaningful combat; teaches resource-loop dependency
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Boot Camp missions 1-2; Campaign opening missions

notable_level_patterns.description: Player must build defensive structures (Pillboxes, turrets) and garrison units to repel structured AI assault; tests learning of defensive doctrine
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Early campaign missions with 'defend your base' secondary objectives

notable_level_patterns.description: Specific mission design leverages faction strengths/weaknesses; Soviet early-game rush capability vs Allied economic scaling creates distinct strategic paths
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Mid-to-late campaign missions where faction choice significantly alters viable tactics

notable_level_patterns.description: Missions requiring capture or destruction of specific non-trivial targets beyond simple base elimination; forces offensive planning and coordinated unit employment
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Unspecified named missions in both Allied and Soviet campaigns
